attributes.heading: Scrambling
attributes.bodyHtml:
Scramble School at a glance

TUITION - $65.00

CLASS SIZE - 12

COURSE DURATION - 2 weeks

Two weekday evening classes

Local Saturday skills training

Full Saturday Backcountry Scramble

HOURS of INSTRUCTION - 16+

Please direct questions to School Directors

Open to Spokane Mountaineer Members Only

Not yet a member?

Join Today!

Scramble School

The 2026 application will open in early July

Course Description:

Scrambling is generally characterized as off-trail travel to gain access to lakes, peaks, or other features not connected

by a maintained trail. Scrambling may be described as “non-technical” climbing that utilizes hands, but not ropes. This

course emphasizes safe travel over rocky terrain, including methods for protection and assistance, when needed.

Outdoor sessions will provide hands on instruction on basic skills, culminating in a Graduation Scramble. Previous

events have tackled Harrison Peak: 7.8 miles round trip w/2600’ gain

Scramble School Director(s) - Michael Pinter and Tyler Nyman

Course Requirements:

Students should be experienced hikers, prepared to hike multiple miles with over 2000’ of gain. Students should

already be familiar with using maps or apps to navigate established trails. This course will focus on off-trail navigation

skills. Backcountry experience preferred.

Required equipment includes:

Climbing Helmet (Mountaineers will have some loaners)

Sturdy hiking shoes or boots (leather recommended)

Course Schedule 2026: (dates tentative)

Lecture I – Thursday, Sept. 24th, 6-8 pm (Saranac Building, downtown)

Outdoor Session I – Saturday, Sept 26th 8 am – 4 pm (Minnehaha)

Lecture II – Thursday, Oct. 1st, 6-8 pm (Saranac Building, downtown)

Outdoor Session I – Saturday, Oct 3rd 8 am – 4 pm (TBD, Pyramid Peak?)

Application Process

The course is limited to 12 participants. All participants must be Spokane Mountaineer Members and 18 years of age

or older. Selection criteria considered will include (but not limited to) application submission date, hiking experience,

course dates commitment, and previous Spokane Mountaineer participation.

The application link below will take you to a form. Please fill this out carefully and completely.
